Let $v$ be the speed of the boat in still water, and let $c$ be the speed of the current.
The distance between the two ports is 60 km.
The time taken to make the round trip is 3 hours and 40 minutes, which is $3 + \frac{40}{60} = 3 + \frac{2}{3} = \frac{9+2}{3} = \frac{11}{3}$ hours.
The distance traveled by the boat is the distance between the two ports, which is 60 km.
The time taken to make the round trip is the distance divided by the speed, so
$$ \frac{60}{v} = \frac{11}{3} $$
Multiplying both sides by $v$, we get
$$ 60 = \frac{11}{3} v $$
Multiplying both sides by $\frac{3}{11}$, we get
$$ v = \frac{3 \times 60}{11} = \frac{180}{11} \approx 16.36 $$
However, we are given that the river's current speed is 3 km/h.
When the boat is traveling against the current, the speed of the boat is $v+3$.
When the boat is traveling with the current, the speed of the boat is $v-3$.
The distance is 60 km, so the time taken is $\frac{60}{v+3} = \frac{11}{3}$.
$$ 60(3) = 11(v+3) $$
$$ 180 = 11v + 33 $$
$$ 11v = 180 - 33 = 147 $$
$$ v = \frac{147}{11} = 13.36 $$
However, we are given that the boat takes 3 hours and 40 minutes to make the round trip.
Let $d$ be the distance between the two ports, which is 60 km.
Let $t$ be the time taken to make the round trip, which is 3 hours and 40 minutes, or $\frac{11}{3}$ hours.
The speed of the boat in still water is $v$.
The current speed is 3 km/h.
The distance traveled by the boat is $d = vt$.
The distance traveled by the boat with the current is $d = vt - 3c$, where $c$ is the speed of the current.
We are given $d = 60$ km, and $t = \frac{11}{3}$ hours.
So $60 = v \cdot \frac{11}{3}$.
$v = \frac{60 \cdot 3}{11} = \frac{180}{11} \approx 16.36$
However, we are given that the river's current speed is 3 km/h.
Let $v$ be the speed of the boat in still water.
The boat is moving with the current, so the effective speed is $v+3$.
The distance is 60 km.
The time taken is $\frac{60}{v+3}$.
We are given that the time taken is 3 hours and 40 minutes, which is $\frac{11}{3}$ hours.
So $\frac{60}{v+3} = \frac{11}{3}$.
$60 \cdot 3 = 11(v+3)$
$180 = 11v + 33$
$11v = 180 - 33 = 147$
$v = \frac{147}{11} \approx 13.36$
The speed of the boat in still water is $\frac{147}{11}$ km/h.
